title: [CRITICAL] Harden daemon.json liveness and project identity checks
description: Deep sweep finding: the daemon CLI start/status/stop paths trust .moe/daemon.json primarily by PID. startDaemon returns early when the recorded pid is alive even if the port is closed or the pid was reused by an unrelated process; stopDaemon can send SIGTERM to that unrelated process if daemon.json has the requested projectPath. moe-proxy also reads daemon.json without checking projectPath, so a stale file can connect MCP calls to the wrong project's daemon. Fix lifecycle validation so PID, project identity, and port/health are verified before reusing or stopping a daemon.
definitionOfDone: Daemon start/status/stop treat daemon.json as valid only when projectPath matches the requested project and the recorded port is reachable/healthy for the Moe daemon.
stopDaemon refuses to signal a process when liveness cannot be tied to the matching Moe daemon, and instead removes stale daemon.json/lock files safely.
Lock-file stale detection avoids being blocked forever by PID reuse where possible.
moe-proxy validates daemon.json projectPath against MOE_PROJECT_PATH/current working directory before connecting and returns a clear JSON-RPC error for mismatches.
Regression tests cover PID-alive/port-closed stale daemon.json, projectPath mismatch, proxy mismatch handling, and normal valid daemon reuse.

implementationPlan.description: Introduce daemon-info validation helpers in packages/moe-daemon/src/index.ts: canonical projectPath comparison, finite port/pid validation, short port or /health probe, and stale-file cleanup. Use them before startDaemon returns early, before status reports running, and before stopDaemon signals a PID. Keep error handling best-effort for malformed files and unlink failures.
implementationPlan.note: Added daemon info validation helpers in index.ts: canonical project path comparison, finite port/pid shape checks, /health probe verifying healthy status and matching projectPath, and validation that also requires PID alive. startDaemon/superviseDaemon reuse daemon.json only after validation; status/stop validate before reporting/killing and stale cleanup removes daemon.json/lock best-effort. Build check: packages/moe-daemon `npm run build` passed after fixing type guards.

implementationPlan.description: Harden lock handling so lock files with dead or unverifiable owners are removed, while PID-reuse ambiguity does not permanently block startup. Add clear logging for stale daemon.json/lock cleanup without exposing stack traces to CLI users.
implementationPlan.note: Hardened daemon.lock handling in index.ts. New locks store JSON {pid, projectPath, createdAt}; legacy PID-only locks still parse. acquireLock removes and retries only when stale/unverifiable (malformed, wrong project, dead owner, or older than MOE_LOCK_STALE_TIMEOUT_MS), and stale daemon cleanup removes locks only when stale rather than blindly deleting a fresh active lock. Logging includes compact reasons without stack traces. Validation: packages/moe-daemon `npm run build` passed.

implementationPlan.description: Update moe-proxy readDaemonInfo paths to validate projectPath against MOE_PROJECT_PATH/process.cwd, reject malformed port/pid, and produce a clear JSON-RPC error instead of connecting to another project's daemon. Keep index.ts and utils.ts behavior consistent to avoid test-only divergence.
implementationPlan.note: Added proxy daemon.json validation with TDD. Red: new utils tests failed because projectPath mismatch and malformed port/pid were accepted. Green: readDaemonInfoResult now validates port/pid/projectPath, rejects mismatched projectPath with clear non-retryable error, and readDaemonInfo returns null for invalid info. Proxy index now uses readDaemonInfoResult and exits with JSON-RPC error instead of connecting/retrying on mismatched/malformed daemon.json. Validation: packages/moe-proxy `npm run build` passed; `npx vitest run src/utils.test.ts src/index.test.ts` => 2 files/36 tests passed.

implementationPlan.description: Add regression tests for daemon CLI helper behavior (extract helpers if needed for testability) and proxy utils: project mismatch, port closed with live PID, malformed daemon.json, and valid daemon info. Run npm test/build in packages/moe-daemon and packages/moe-proxy.
implementationPlan.note: Added daemon lifecycle regression tests for valid health/project identity, live PID with closed port, wrong-project daemon.json, malformed daemon info, safe stopDaemon cleanup/no SIGTERM, and stale PID-reuse-ambiguous locks. Added proxy tests for wrong-project and malformed daemon.json including JSON-RPC non-retryable error path. Validation: packages/moe-daemon npm test 25 files/412 tests passed; npm run build passed. packages/moe-proxy npm test 2 files/36 tests passed; npm run build passed.

reopenReason: Rejecting because daemon validation still does not tie the recorded PID to the process serving the healthy Moe port. This leaves the critical PID-reuse stop/start/status risk partially unfixed even though the test suites pass.

planningNotes.approachesConsidered: Rejected command-line inspection of arbitrary PIDs as the only solution because it is platform-specific and brittle. Prefer projectPath match + port open/health response for reuse; for stop, avoid killing when health/project validation fails.
planningNotes.codebaseInsights: VS Code and JetBrains already compare daemonInfo.projectPath to the current workspace and often probe the port. The daemon CLI/proxy should use a shared project-path comparison and port/health probe instead of raw process-alive checks alone.
planningNotes.risks: Be careful not to hang start/stop on long network checks; reuse existing short socket timeout constants. Tests should mock port checks and process liveness rather than killing real processes. Include error handling and tests for malformed daemon.json.

failedDodItems: stopDaemon refuses to signal a process when liveness cannot be tied to the matching Moe daemon, and instead removes stale daemon.json/lock files safely.
Regression tests cover PID-alive/port-closed stale daemon.json, projectPath mismatch, proxy mismatch handling, and normal valid daemon reuse.

issues.description: `probeDaemonHealth` validates only /health status/projectPath while `validateDaemonInfoForProject` separately checks daemon.json.pid is alive. A stale daemon.json can point at a live unrelated/reused PID and a different healthy Moe port for the same project, causing `stopDaemon` to signal the unrelated PID. Include pid in /health and require it to equal daemon.json.pid before reuse/status/stop.

Add a regression for the missing PID-to-port tie: use a separate live PID (spawned child is fine) plus a mock healthy matching-project /health server from the test process, then assert validation is invalid and `stopDaemon` does not signal that PID. It should fail before adding pid equality to health validation.
